description: Benchmark threshold configuration for CI regression detection (hosted-runner profile)

indexTimePerFile._comment: Raised for LadybugDB graph backend: graph writes are inherently slower than SQLite inserts. CI runners see ~2000-2500ms per file.

indexTimePerSymbol._comment: Raised for LadybugDB graph backend: per-symbol graph operations cost more than SQLite row inserts. CI runners see ~150-200ms per symbol.

edgesPerSymbol._comment: Provider-first SCIP on locked zod-oss produced ~0.53 edges/symbol on hosted Linux after semantic enrichment became default; keep a nonzero quality floor without requiring the legacy edge-density shape.

graphConnectivity._comment: Provider-first SCIP on locked zod-oss produced ~0.288 graph connectivity on hosted Linux; keep this just below the observed value to catch material connectivity regressions.

exportedSymbolRatio._comment: Widened from 10 %: TS call-resolver indexes more internal helper symbols (alias targets, barrel re-exports), which legitimately dilutes the exported ratio.

sliceBuildTimeMs._comment: Raised from 350 ms: hosted Linux runners have produced valid zod-oss native-addon samples around 420 ms after edge-density increases, while this still guards against subsecond slice regressions.
